title: Model Routing Strategies to Cut AI Dev Tool Costs
excerpt: Not every task needs the most capable model. Practical routing rules that reduce AI coding spend without hurting developer velocity.
bodyHtml:
ForgeMeter customers often find backend teams account for the largest share of AI spend—not because they code more, but because they default to the most capable (and expensive) models for every prompt.

Match model to task

TaskTypical routing
Boilerplate generationEconomy / fast model
Code review commentsMid-tier model
Cross-repo refactorsPremium model with context limits
Test generationEconomy model with lint feedback

Operationalize routing

  1. Baseline spend by team and model for two weeks
  2. Identify top three expensive workflows (reviews, tests, docs)
  3. Pilot down-tier models with quality spot-checks
  4. Document defaults in team playbooks—not ad hoc per developer

Let data suggest the swap

Generic advice helps; your data closes the loop. ForgeMeter's AI summaries call out concrete swaps—e.g., routing code reviews to a haiku-class model to save ~$180/month for a single squad.

Explore recommendations on the ForgeMeter live demo or connect your org for personalized insights.
